No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to eradicating poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to health and well-being outcomes.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides tertiary education (4.3), equips students with technical skills for employment (4.4), and explicitly addresses 'sustainability issues' (4.7) as a core competency.

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to clean water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course provides data analysis and programming skills, supporting technological upgrading, innovation, and decent job creation (Targets 8.2, 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course focuses on ICT, data management, programming, and information security, upgrading technological capabilities and encouraging innovation (Targets 9.5, 9.c).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competency B explicitly addresses 'sustainability issues,' aligning with promoting awareness for sustainable development (Target 12.8).

Climate Action.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course covers 'information security, privacy, and ethics,' contributing to accountable institutions (16.6) and protecting fundamental freedoms (16.10).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds capacity in data analysis and programming, essential for reliable data (17.18) and enhancing ICT use (17.8) for SDG monitoring.
